center.name: 撒哈拉沙漠
center.summary: 撒哈拉沙漠是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联45种鸟类，包括鸵鸟、白臉樹鴨、Black guinea fowl等45种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in most of Africa south of the Sahara

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It generally inhabits open forest and savanna, and is a locally common resident breeder in Africa south of the Sahara

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black bishop (Euplectes gierowii) is a species of passerine bird in the family Ploceidae native to Africa south of the Sahara. Three subspecies are recognised

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in most of sub-Saharan Africa except in very arid areas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black guinea fowl is native to the western portions of Central Africa (south of the Sahara), from Nigeria and Cameroon in the west, east and southeast through Equatorial Guinea, Gabon, Angola, Central African Republic, Republic of the Congo, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The geographical limit of the species' range is estimated to be around . The birds are terrestrial, however they can fly short distances

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The desert sparrow (Passer simplex) is a species of bird in the sparrow family Passeridae, found in the Sahara Desert of northern Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in the savanna region south of the Sahara from Mali eastwards as far as Ethiopia and north-west Kenya

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in dry open woodlands and moist savanna in Africa south of the Sahara, but is absent from the equatorial forest belt

birds.evidence: The Horus swift (Apus horus) is a small bird in the swift family Apodidae that is found in sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It winters in Western Africa south of the Sahara Desert

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in much of Africa south of the Sahara, and Madagascar

birds.evidence: Avian Biol.]] 26: 139–153. The species is migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-throated bee-eater (Merops albicollis) is a species of bee-eater that breeds in semi-desert along the southern edge of the Sahara

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-winged widowbird (Euplectes albonotatus) is a species of passerine bird in the family Ploceidae native to Africa south of the Sahara. It is highly sexually dimorphic in its breeding season, during which the male's yellow plumage turns dark and he gains more white feathers, contrasting with the female's predominantly pale coloration

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|in Namibia thumb|in Uganda thumb|In The Gambia thumb The woodland kingfisher (Halcyon senegalensis) is a tree kingfisher that is widely distributed in Africa south of the Sahara

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: taha) displaying near Lake Baringo, Kenya, and a captive female (nominate race) | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Euplectes | species = afer | authority = (Gmelin, 1789) | synonyms = Taha afer }} The yellow-crowned bishop (Euplectes afer) is a species of passerine bird in the family Ploceidae native to Africa south of the Sahara. It is highly sexually dimorphic in its breeding season, during which the male adopts a distinctive yellow and black plumage, contrasting with the female's predominantly brown coloration

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa.{{cite book|title=Phylogeny and biogeography of the genus Sylvia, in: Shirihai, Hadoram: Sylvia warblers: 24-29

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Birds from Eurasia spend the winter in sub-Saharan Africa, while those from the eastern part of the range migrate to the Indian subcontinent

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cristatus, Lake Baringo, Kenya | genus = Corythornis | species = cristatus | authority = (Pallas, 1764) |subdivision_ranks=Subspecies | subdivision = See text | synonyms = Alcedo cristata }} The malachite kingfisher (Corythornis cristatus) is a river kingfisher which is widely distributed in Africa south of the Sahara

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is a desert bird which is found across parts of the Sahara from Mauritania to central Sudan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to Africa, mainly south of the Sahara, and has been widely introduced, as a domesticated species, into the West Indies, North America, Colombia, Brazil,{{cite web |title=Tô-fraco, galinha d'angola, capota ou cocá?

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The orange-breasted waxbill is found in grassland and savannahs south of the Sahara in Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It winters in central Africa and Arabia, south of the Sahara Desert but north of the Equator, from Senegal east to Yemen

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The southern limits are northwestern Africa, Iraq, Iran and the northwestern Himalayas. This nightjar formerly bred in Syria and Lebanon. All populations are migratory, and most birds winter in Africa south of the Sahara, with just a few records from Pakistan, Morocco and Israel

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in southernmost Europe and sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Sedge warblers are migratory, crossing the Sahara to get from their European and Asian breeding grounds to spend winter in Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Pharaoh eagle-owl is native to much of arid Northern Africa, including some of the most desolate parts of the central Saharan Desert, where it may be found in Algeria, Chad, Egypt, Eritrea, Libya, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Niger, Senegal (vagrant owls), Sudan and Tunisia. Its range extends eastward into the Arabian Peninsula and the Middle East, where it is found in Iran, Iraq, Israel, Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Palestine, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria and the United Arab Emirates. Its preferred habitat is mostly open, barren plains or desert with scattered palms or other endemic plants, where it nests in rocky outcrops, wadis and cliffs

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The western desert wheatear breeds in the Sahara and the northern Arabian Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This small passerine bird is strongly migratory, wintering in Africa just south of the Sahara, Arabia and India

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is strongly migratory, all populations wintering in Africa south of the Sahara

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-faced whistling duck (Dendrocygna viduata) is a whistling duck that breeds in sub-Saharan Africa and much of South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in a band across Africa between the Sahara and the Equator

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Well scattered groups of breeding pelicans occur through Eurasia from the eastern Mediterranean to Vietnam. In Eurasia, fresh or brackish waters may be inhabited and the pelicans may be found in lakes, deltas, lagoons and marshes, usually with dense reed beds nearby for nesting purposes. Additionally, sedentary populations are found year-round in Africa, south of the Sahara Desert although these are patchy

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the eastern savannah of sub-Saharan Africa, from the Central African Republic east to South Sudan and south to northern and eastern South Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds across Europe into the temperate western Palaearctic where it is migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa or Arabia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|At an official bird ringing station The western house martin breeds across temperate Eurosiberia east to central Mongolia and the Yenisei River, and in Morocco, Tunisia and northern Algeria, and migrates on a broad front to winter in sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The brown babbler inhabits the broad band of the Sahel between the Sahara Desert and the tropical forests of Western Africa, from southern Mauritania, Senegal and the Gambia to southern Sudan, Uganda and western Kenya

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in northern and sub-Saharan Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Common ostriches formerly occupied Africa north and south of the Sahara, East Africa, Africa south of the rainforest belt, and much of Asia Minor. Today common ostriches prefer open land and are native to the savannas and Sahel of Africa, both north and south of the equatorial forest zone. In southwest Africa they inhabit the semi-desert or true desert

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is widespread south of the Sahara and also occurs in Madagascar

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Although the British climate is sub-optimal, compensatory factors include the ready availability of food, (particularly from bird tables), a shorter migration distance, and the avoidance of the Alps and the Sahara Desert. It was originally thought that most of these wintering birds come from Germany, and isotope analysis (which enables the wintering location to be determined) showed that the continental birds wintering in Britain tend to mate only among themselves, and do not usually interbreed with those wintering in the Mediterranean or western Africa
